question: Of the cities within the United States where U.S. presidents were born, which two are the farthest apart from the westernmost to the easternmost going east, giving the city names only? Give them to me in alphabetical order, in a comma-separated list

research_queries: US presidents birthplaces cities complete list
all US presidents where were they born city state
westernmost easternmost US president birth cities coordinates
working_plan: Research all US presidents and their exact birth cities
Get geographic coordinates or longitude data for each birth city
Use code to identify the westernmost and easternmost cities
Return the two cities in alphabetical order, comma-separated
planner.answer_shape: comma-separated list of two city names

solver.reasoning_summary: Analyzed all US presidential birth cities from comprehensive sources. Honolulu, Hawaii (Obama's birthplace) is clearly the westernmost at ~-157.8° longitude. Among eastern cities, Braintree, Massachusetts (birthplace of John Adams and John Quincy Adams) is the easternmost at ~-71.0° longitude, making these two cities the farthest apart from west to east. Listed in alphabetical order as requested.

arguments.code:
# Extract president birth cities from the Wikipedia data
president_birth_cities = [
    ("George Washington", "Popes Creek", "Virginia"),
    ("John Adams", "Braintree", "Massachusetts"),
    ("Thomas Jefferson", "Shadwell", "Virginia"),
    ("James Madison", "Port Conway", "Virginia"),
    ("James Monroe", "Monroe Hall", "Virginia"),
    ("Andrew Jackson", "Waxhaws Region", "South Carolina"),
    ("John Quincy Adams", "Braintree", "Massachusetts"),
    ("William Henry Harrison", "Charles City County", "Virginia"),
    ("Martin Van Buren", "Kinderhook", "New York"),
    ("Zachary Taylor", "Barboursville", "Virginia"),
    ("John Tyler", "Charles City County", "Virginia"),
    ("James Buchanan", "Cove Gap", "Pennsylvania"),
    ("James K. Polk", "Pineville", "North Carolina"),
    ("Millard Fillmore", "Summerhill", "New York"),
    ("Franklin Pierce", "Hillsborough", "New Hampshire"),
    ("Andrew Johnson", "Raleigh", "North Carolina"),
    ("Abraham Lincoln", "Hodgenville", "Kentucky"),
    ("Ulysses S. Grant", "Point Pleasant", "Ohio"),
    ("Rutherford B. Hayes", "Delaware", "Ohio"),
    ("Chester A. Arthur", "Fairfield", "Vermont"),
    ("James A. Garfield", "Moreland Hills", "Ohio"),
    ("Benjamin Harrison", "North Bend", "Ohio"),
    ("Grover Cleveland", "Caldwell", "New Jersey"),
    ("William McKinley", "Niles", "Ohio"),
    ("Woodrow Wilson", "Staunton", "Virginia"),
    ("William Howard Taft", "Cincinnati", "Ohio"),
    ("Theodore Roosevelt", "Gramercy Park", "New York"), # This is New York City
    ("Warren G. Harding", "Blooming Grove", "Ohio"),
    ("Calvin Coolidge", "Plymouth", "Vermont"),
    ("Herbert Hoover", "West Branch", "Iowa"),
    ("Franklin D. Roosevelt", "Hyde Park", "New York"),
    ("Harry S. Truman", "Lamar", "Missouri"),
    ("Dwight D. Eisenhower", "Denison", "Texas"),
    ("Lyndon B. Johnson", "Stonewall", "Texas"),
    ("Ronald Reagan", "Tampico", "Illinois"),
    ("Richard Nixon", "Yorba Linda", "California"),
    ("Gerald Ford", "Omaha", "Nebraska"),
    ("John F. Kennedy", "Brookline", "Massachusetts"),
    ("George H. W. Bush", "Milton", "Massachusetts"),
    ("Jimmy Carter", "Plains", "Georgia"),
    ("Joe Biden", "Scranton", "Pennsylvania"),
    ("Donald Trump", "Queens", "New York"), # This is New York City
    ("George W. Bush", "New Haven", "Connecticut"),
    ("Bill Clinton", "Hope", "Arkansas"),
    ("Barack Obama", "Honolulu", "Hawaii")
]

# Extract unique cities (some presidents were born in the same city)
unique_cities = set()
for president, city, state in president_birth_cities:
    # Handle special cases
    if city == "Gramercy Park" or city == "Queens":
        city = "New York City"
    if city == "Braintree":  # Modern Quincy, MA
        city = "Quincy"
    unique_cities.add((city, state))

unique_cities = sorted(list(unique_cities))
print("Unique birth cities:")
for city, state in unique_cities:
    print(f"{city}, {state}")
print(f"\nTotal unique cities: {len(unique_cities)}")

stdout=Unique birth cities:

Blooming Grove, Ohio
Brookline, Massachusetts

2. Opened "List of presidents of the United States by home state" on Wikipedia.
3. Searched the eastern cities to find the easternmost one (Braintree, MA).
